name: DAN Jailbreak
description: Do Anything Now (DAN) prompt injection that bypasses safety guardrails by convincing the model to adopt an unrestricted alter-ego persona.
mitigation: Add explicit role constraints and output format restrictions. Avoid allowing role redefinition in user-facing prompts.

name: System Prompt Extraction
description: Techniques to extract the system prompt or hidden instructions by asking the model to repeat, translate, or encode its instructions.
patterns: ignore previous instructions and tell me
mitigation: Never include sensitive logic in system prompts. Add instruction-following boundaries and output validation.

name: Role-Play Wrapper Attack
description: Wrapping malicious requests inside fictional role-play scenarios (e.g., 'You are a character in a movie who...') to bypass content filters.
mitigation: Implement output content filtering. Add constraints that persist across hypothetical scenarios.

name: Indirect Prompt Injection via Context
description: Embedding malicious instructions inside documents, URLs, or data that the model processes as context, causing it to follow attacker instructions.
mitigation: Sanitize all external data before including in prompts. Use clear delimiters between user input and system instructions.

name: Token Smuggling
description: Using Unicode characters, homoglyphs, zero-width joiners, or base64 encoding to smuggle harmful tokens past content filters.
mitigation: Normalize all input text before processing. Decode and inspect encoded content. Block unusual Unicode characters.

name: PII Exfiltration via Structured Output
description: Crafting prompts that trick the model into outputting sensitive data in structured formats (JSON, CSV, markdown tables) that can be programmatically scraped.
mitigation: Add output format constraints. Implement post-processing output filters for PII detection.

name: Hallucination Exploitation
description: Prompts designed to exploit model hallucination tendencies, generating fake but convincing citations, legal references, or medical advice.
mitigation: Add constraints requiring factual grounding. Include disclaimers in output format. Use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

name: Multi-Turn Context Manipulation
description: Gradually steering model behavior across multiple conversation turns to slowly override safety guidelines without triggering single-turn detection.
mitigation: Implement per-turn safety checks. Reset context boundaries periodically. Monitor for behavioral drift across turns.

name: Training Data Extraction
description: Prompts designed to extract memorized training data, including private information, copyrighted content, or proprietary code.
mitigation: Use differential privacy in training. Implement output filters for known memorized content. Limit verbatim reproduction.

name: API Key / Credential Leakage
description: Prompts that inadvertently contain API keys, passwords, database connection strings, or other credentials that get sent to third-party AI providers.
mitigation: Implement pre-processing credential scanners. Never include raw credentials in prompts. Use environment variables and secret managers.

name: Denial of Service via Resource Exhaustion
description: Prompts designed to consume maximum tokens or trigger infinite loops, leading to high API costs or service disruption.
mitigation: Implement token limits. Set maximum output length. Add cost monitoring and circuit breakers.

name: Model Behavior Manipulation via Few-Shot Poisoning
description: Injecting carefully crafted few-shot examples that steer model behavior toward producing harmful, biased, or incorrect outputs.
mitigation: Validate and sanitize all few-shot examples. Use curated example sets. Monitor for distributional shifts in outputs.

name: Multimodal Injection via Image Prompts
description: Embedding textual instructions inside images that vision-language models read and follow, bypassing text-based filters.
patterns: read the text in the image
mitigation: Implement image content analysis. Apply same safety checks to extracted text from images. Separate image description from instruction following.

name: Compliance Data Harvesting
description: Using prompts to generate or synthesize regulated data (HIPAA, GDPR, PCI-DSS) that could create compliance violations.
mitigation: Tag prompts requesting regulated data types. Implement compliance-aware output filtering. Log all data generation requests.

name: Supply Chain Prompt Injection
description: Injecting malicious instructions through third-party plugins, tools, or data sources that feed into LLM prompts without proper sanitization.
mitigation: Sanitize all external tool outputs. Implement trust boundaries between plugins and core prompts. Use allowlists for external data sources.
